channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — Officials were investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead waited anxiously Saturday for answers.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started, an attempt by President Donald Trump's administration to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown dragged into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Trump, AstraZeneca unveil drug pricing breaks for US consumers
item.description: The president said the U.K.-based company will list many of its most popular drugs online at TrumpRx, a new website.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Missing mom found alive in forest as officials investigate ‘suspicious’ house fire
item.description: Britney Gard, 46, called 911 from a forest near Bainbridge, Indiana, ending a week-long search that began after her house caught fire under suspicious circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the fraud case against New York attorney general Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James, a longtime political foe of President Donald Trump, was indicted by a federal grand jury Thursday on mortgage fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: BY PHILIP MARCELO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Connecticut man, woman charged with 31 counts of animal cruelty after dead animals found in trailer, bus
item.description: "That's crazy, man," Julius Bogues, who lives in Waterbury, said. "We ride by here and you never know what's going on right around you every day, very bizarre.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, NewsNation

item.title: Troops will miss paychecks next week without action on the government shutdown
item.description: The nation’s third shutdown in 12 years is once again raising anxiety levels among service members and their families because those in uniform are working without pay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of man who died on Stardust Racers demands its closure after new reports of malfunction
item.description: The family of Kevin Rodriguez Zavala, the Epic Universe park visitor who died after riding the Stardust Racers roller coaster, issued a statement demanding that the ride be closed after new reports of restraint malfunctions.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: Officials say an explosion at a Tennessee explosives plant has left 18 people missing and feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rutgers professor signs petition to disband Turning Point USA from campus
item.description: A Rutgers professor signed a Change.org petition accusing the conservative Turning Point USA chapter of promoting hate speech and inciting violence against the community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: The White House is responding bitterly to President Donald Trump being passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize despite efforts from Republicans and world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Mike Pesoli, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Pressure grows on Johnson to hold vote on military pay
item.description: Pressure is rising on House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to call the House back in session for a vote on ensuring military pay during the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: A history of President Trump's legal battles with New York Attorney General Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The day she was elected New York's attorney general, Letitia James blasted President Donald Trump as a “con man” and “carnival barker" and pledged to scrutinize his public policies and personal business dealings.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, ABC 4

item.title: Most states with National Guard troops in DC plan to withdraw this fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than half the states contributing National Guard troops to President Donald Trump's federal law enforcement initiative in Washington have set target dates for their withdrawal later this fall, state officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico officials respond to billboards showing state's worst national rankings
item.description: "While I applauded everything positive New Mexico does to improve childcare and college tuition- the fact remains that 80% of eighth grader cannot read or do math.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Hoops, NewsNation

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgment
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Top DHS official defends ICE agents filmed shooting pepper ball at pastor's head
item.description: (The Hill) – Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin on Wednesday defended an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer who shot a pastor with a pepper ball from a rooftop after video of the incident went viral this week. Videos of the Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Political Power Ranker: Analyzing this year's biggest elections
item.description: On this week's edition of Political Power Ranker, we're breaking down some of the biggest races across the country.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Turning Point USA announces 'All American' Super Bowl halftime show
item.description: Turning Point USA, the grassroots conservative political action network founded by the late activist Charlie Kirk, is planning a Super Bowl halftime show with the goal of competing with the official show featuring Bad Bunny and sponsored by Apple Music.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas accepts Trump peace plan ending war in Gaza and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats win momentum over GOP in shutdown fight
item.description: As the shutdown fight stretches into its second week, the winds have shifted in favor of the Democrats — at least for the moment. Early polls say voters are more likely to blame President Trump and the Republicans for the lengthy impasse.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas National Guard troops now protecting federal property in Chicago area, official says
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A small number of National Guard troops has started protecting federal property in the Chicago area and assisting law enforcement in Memphis on Wednesday, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Katie Porter cuts CBS interview after question on Trump voters
item.description: “I don’t want to keep doing this. I’m gonna call it. Thank you," Porter said after several rounds of questioning about her odds.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Bears' Tyrique Stevenson set to return to Washington year after embarrassing loss
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — Tyrique Stevenson says he’s ready to return to the scene of one of the most embarrassing moments in NFL history Monday night when the Chicago Bears visit Washington.
item.dc:creator: GENE CHAMBERLAIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump considers cutting US refugee intake to 7,500, focusing on white South Africans, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is considering admitting far fewer refugees into the U.S. this year — as few as 7,500, and mostly white South Africans, officials say — a dramatic new low as the administration is conducting sweeping immigration raids as part of its mass deportation agenda.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
